examples: She gave him a pitying look; that was the sort of excuse her father would use.
Dickon followed her with a queer, almost pitying, look on his face.
This kind-hearted girl, pitying the misery of Toad, said to her father one day, "Father!"
This is confessional poetry at its scrupulous best: neither self-dramatising nor self-pitying, but penetrating, focused and unflinching.
If Richard Nixon talked like that, they'd have called him paranoid and self-pitying.
